description: Race harness as a real test, not a gate. Closes ROADMAP P1 'Race harness as a real test, not a gate.' Today `internal/s3api/race_test.go` is only a sanity unit/integration check — never run for ≥1h against the full make up-all stack. Cycle scope: carve out `internal/racetest` library, ship `cmd/strata-racecheck` standalone binary, extend workload to multipart + versioning + conditional + DeleteObjects, add a verifier with read-after-write + listing-convergence oracles, ship a memory-tuned compose `ci` profile that fits ubuntu-latest (2 vCPU / 7 GB RAM), wire `make race-soak`, run nightly via GitHub Actions on free runners, and produce a structured JSON-lines report + Markdown summary mirrored to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY. NOT in scope: chaos / fault injection, replication / multi-region scenarios, paid runners, notification plumbing, regression-pinning the baseline. 9 stories. Closes ROADMAP P1 on cycle close.

userStories.title: Carve out internal/racetest library package
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the race harness logic in a reusable package so it can be driven by both `go test` and a standalone binary.
acceptanceCriteria: Move shared workload helpers (raceFixture, newRaceClient, raceWorkload) from `internal/s3api/race_test.go` into a new `internal/racetest/` package
Existing TestRace* tests in `internal/s3api/` import from `internal/racetest/` and still pass under `go test -race`
No semantic change to the workload mix; only relocation + minimal exported-API surface
`internal/racetest/` exposes `Run(ctx, cfg) (*Report, error)` accepting `Config{HTTPEndpoint, Duration, Concurrency, BucketCount, ObjectKeys, VerifyEvery}` and returning a structured Report
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Carved out internal/racetest package: Fixture/RunScenario/VerifyInvariants for in-process tests + Config/Report/Run(ctx, cfg) for external endpoint. internal/s3api/race_test.go and race_integration_test.go are now thin wrappers wiring per-backend Fixture and delegating workload+invariants. Added racetest/runner_test.go smoke test asserting Run populates OpsByClass and rejects bad config (HTTPEndpoint empty / Duration 0 / Concurrency 0 / Concurrency > MaxConcurrency=64). Anonymous HTTP at this stage; SigV4 wiring is US-002. Workload mix unchanged from pre-cycle (PUT/DELETE/Multipart 3-way switch); extension to versioning/conditional/DeleteObjects is US-003.

userStories.title: cmd/strata-racecheck standalone binary
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to drive the race harness against an already-running gateway without building the full Go test binary, so CI can target a containerised stack.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `cmd/strata-racecheck/main.go` that calls `racetest.Run()`
Flags: --endpoint, --duration, --concurrency, --buckets, --keys-per-bucket, --report=<path>, --access-key, --secret-key, --region
Process exit code: 0 on no inconsistencies, 1 on inconsistencies, 2 on transport / setup error
Writes a JSON-lines report file (one event per line: op_started, op_done, inconsistency, summary)
Prints a human-readable summary to stdout at end
Builds without `-tags ceph` — talks pure HTTP, no librados linkage
`cmd/strata-racecheck` added to `make build` alongside strata and strata-admin
Update `tasks/prd-binary-consolidation.md` Non-Goals: strata-racecheck is a developer/CI tool, NOT a regression on the two-binary goal
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added cmd/strata-racecheck/main.go with flags --endpoint, --duration, --concurrency, --buckets, --keys-per-bucket, --report, --access-key, --secret-key, --region. Exit codes 0 (clean) / 1 (inconsistencies) / 2 (setup-failure). JSON-lines events (op_started/op_done/inconsistency/summary) emitted by internal/racetest via new EventSink (file or io.Writer). SigV4 signing wired via aws-sdk-go-v2 v4.NewSigner — empty AccessKey falls through to anonymous mode for in-process tests. internal/racetest gained Config.AccessKey/SecretKey/Region/ReportPath/EventWriter, signer.go (cred wrapper), events.go (sink). Makefile build target now produces bin/strata-racecheck alongside strata + strata-admin. Binary-consolidation migration doc gained Non-Goals section documenting strata-racecheck exception (developer/CI tool, not a production binary). Workload extension to versioning/conditional/DeleteObjects + verifier oracle stays in US-003/US-004.

userStories.title: Workload extension — multipart + versioning + delete
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the race harness to exercise the full high-risk surface so the ≥1h run covers the operations historically prone to LWT bugs.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing workload (PUT/GET/DELETE/list interleave) preserved
New op classes: MultipartCreate+Upload+Complete, EnableVersioning flip mid-load, ConditionalPut(IfNoneMatch), DeleteObjects batch
Workload weights configurable via `Config.Mix map[string]float64`
Default mix exercises every op class at least 5% of the time so a ≥1h run hits each at least 50× per worker
Race detector (`go test -race`) clean against in-memory backend with the new ops
Versioning flips weighted at ≥10% per Technical Considerations note (Cassandra LWT hot path)
Streaming SigV4 path included in workload at ~50/50 weight with pre-computed-SHA path
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Workload extension: Config gained Mix map[string]float64 + StreamingRatio float64; Run dispatches via weighted opPicker over 8 op classes (put/get/delete/list/multipart/versioning_flip/conditional_put/delete_objects). DefaultMix sums to 1.0 with every class >=5% and versioning_flip = 10%. Streaming SigV4 path implemented in signer.signStreaming: aws-sdk-go-v2 v4.SignHTTP with payloadHash=STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD, then chained-HMAC chunk encoding (encodeStreamingBody/computeChunkSignature) using deriveSigningKey duplicated locally to avoid internal/auth dep cycle. Body-carrying ops (put + conditional_put) route through doPUTBody which picks streaming vs fixed-payload at StreamingRatio (default 0.5). Streaming auto-disabled when signer is nil (anonymous in-process tests). Exposed const Op* identifiers and DefaultMix as public surface. Pre-existing race in internal/meta/memory.ListObjects (read of versions[0] outside RLock) surfaced once OpList joined the harness; fixed by holding RLock through scan. New tests: TestRunStreamingSigV4 (auth.ModeRequired gateway, StreamingRatio=1.0, asserts streaming PUT + conditional PUT succeed end-to-end); TestRunRejectsBadConfig extended with StreamingRatio>1 and negative-mix-weight cases; TestRunSmoke now asserts every default-mix class > 0; TestRunSmokeBinary stdout assertion extended to all 8 classes. cmd/strata-racecheck printSummary lists all 8 classes in canonical order.

userStories.title: Inconsistency oracle — read-after-write + listing convergence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the harness to assert specific invariants beyond `go race` so silent data divergence shows up in the report.
acceptanceCriteria: After every successful PUT, harness records expected (bucket, key, version_id, etag, size) tuple
Verifier goroutine periodically GETs the latest version and compares ETag + size; mismatch = `inconsistency` report event
After every EnableVersioning flip, subsequent PUTs produce monotonically increasing version_id per key (verifier asserts via ListObjectVersions)
After every DeleteObjects batch, deleted keys do NOT reappear in a follow-up ListObjects for the same prefix within configurable grace window (default 5s)
Report JSON-lines includes `inconsistency` events with full diagnostic payload (expected vs observed, request_id, timestamp)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Verifier oracle implemented across three new files (tracker.go, verifier.go, oracle_test.go) plus runner.go wiring. Tracker collects per-(bucket,key) state: history ring (cap 32) for diagnostic strings, unbounded allEtags map for read-after-write validation, deletedAt for delete-grace tracking, versionIDs slice for versioning monotonicity. Workload feeds tracker via RecordIntent (md5 of body pre-flight, absorbs the response→Record race window), RecordPut, RecordDelete, RecordBatchDelete. Verifier ticks every Config.VerifyEvery (default 1s), runs three oracles per bucket: (1) checkReadAfterWrite samples ≤8 keys, validates GET etag in tracker; persistent mismatch loop (5 × 100ms) absorbs transient peer-worker races; (2) checkVersioningPresence picks one tracked key per bucket and asserts every recorded version_id appears in ?versions response (skipped on buckets without EnableVersioning); (3) checkDeleteGrace asserts pending-expired deletes (deletedAt + grace ≤ now) are absent from ListObjectsV2; HEAD-confirms before flagging since tracker.deletedAt is cleared in worker-call order, not gateway-commit order — HEAD=200 absorbs the race, anything else (404, 405 for delete-marker latest) is the real divergence. Run drains for grace+verEvery after Duration ends so deletes have time to age past grace and the final pass surfaces them. Inconsistencies emit JSON-lines events with Kind/Bucket/Key/Detail/Expected/Observed/RequestID/Timestamp; Snapshot() returns a copy for Report.Inconsistencies. New Config knobs: VerifyEvery (Duration; <0 disables verifier), DeleteGracePeriod (Duration; default 5s; <0 rejected). Tests: TestRunCleanRunNoInconsistencies (clean run produces zero false positives), TestVerifierFlagsETagMismatch + TestVerifierFlagsResurrectedDelete + TestVerifierFlagsMissingVersion (proxy that injects each fault class and asserts ≥1 corresponding inconsistency), TestTrackerValidateETag (tracker unit), TestRejectsNegativeGrace (config validation). Memory backend ListObjectVersions race fix: hold RLock through full scan (mirror of US-003 ListObjects fix) — concurrent PutObject mutates per-key version slices in place, so reading versions[i] after dropping the lock is racy under -race once the verifier's checkVersioningPresence joins the harness.

userStories.title: Compose ci profile — stack tuned for 7 GB / 2 vCPU
userStories.description: As a CI maintainer, I want a `ci`-profile compose configuration that fits the full stack into ubuntu-latest without OOM.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `deploy/docker/docker-compose.ci.yml` override with: osd_memory_target=1073741824 (1 GiB) on Ceph OSD; Cassandra MAX_HEAP_SIZE=2G HEAP_NEWSIZE=400M; Ceph MGR dashboard module disabled; single replica everywhere; Cassandra cluster_replication SimpleStrategy/RF=1; Prometheus + Grafana removed from `ci`-profile up-set
`make up-all-ci` brings up trimmed stack via `docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.ci.yml --profile ci up -d`
Stack reaches `/readyz` 200 within 8 min on ubuntu-latest (CI smoke step times out at 10 min)
Idle stack RSS post-readyz pre-load ≤ 5 GB total
Existing `make up-all` (full-fat) unchanged; `ci` is purely additive
`docker compose config -q` validates clean against override
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added deploy/docker/docker-compose.ci.yml override + Makefile up-all-ci/wait-strata targets + bootstrap.sh extensions for OSD_MEMORY_TARGET / MGR_DASHBOARD_DISABLE. Override sets Cassandra MAX_HEAP_SIZE=2G HEAP_NEWSIZE=400M (vs base 1G/128M — race-soak sustained-write needs more heap to avoid GC starvation), Ceph MEMSTORE_BYTES=1Gi + OSD_MEMORY_TARGET=1Gi (vs base 4Gi memstore), MGR_DASHBOARD_DISABLE=1, and gates prometheus + grafana behind the `full` profile so `--profile ci` skips them. bootstrap.sh applies osd_memory_target via `ceph config set osd osd_memory_target` after the cluster is healthy and `ceph mgr module disable dashboard` when DASHBOARD_DISABLE=1; both are no-ops on the developer stack (env unset). Cassandra RF=1 SimpleStrategy is already the default in deploy/strata.toml + internal/config/config.go — no change needed. Single replica everywhere is satisfied by base shape (one cassandra/one ceph/one strata). Validated `docker compose -f base -f ci.yml --profile ci config --services` returns exactly cassandra/ceph/strata; base-only config returns the unchanged 5-service set so `make up-all` is preserved. `go vet ./...` + `go build ./...` + full `go test ./...` all green. Compose schema validates clean (`docker compose config -q` exits 0). Stack-startup-time + 5 GB RSS budget claims are CI-runtime checks — verifiable on first `make up-all-ci` exercise from US-006/US-007.

userStories.title: make race-soak target + script
userStories.description: As an operator, I want one command to bring up the full stack and run the race harness for ≥1 hour against it.
acceptanceCriteria: New `scripts/racecheck/run.sh`: brings up stack via `make up-all-ci` if CI=true else `make up-all`; waits for `/readyz` (ceiling 10 min); runs `bin/strata-racecheck --duration=${RACE_DURATION:-1h} --concurrency=${RACE_CONCURRENCY:-32} --report=report/race.jsonl --endpoint=http://localhost:9999`; captures docker logs for strata, strata-cassandra, strata-ceph into report/; emits df -h / + free -m before/after into report/host.txt; exits with harness's exit code
New `make race-soak` target invoking the script
Default duration (1h) and concurrency (32) overridable via RACE_DURATION= / RACE_CONCURRENCY= env vars
Concurrency cap: harness refuses to start with --concurrency > 64
Script idempotent — re-running clears prior report/ directory
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added scripts/racecheck/run.sh + Makefile race-soak target. Script idempotently scrubs report/ (rm -rf + mkdir), builds bin/strata-racecheck if missing, conditionally brings up the stack (CI=true → make up-all-ci, else make up-all; STACK_UP=0 skips bring-up for operator iteration), polls /readyz on RACE_ENDPOINT (default http://localhost:9999) with READYZ_TIMEOUT_SEC ceiling (default 600s), invokes bin/strata-racecheck with --duration=${RACE_DURATION:-1h} --concurrency=${RACE_CONCURRENCY:-32} --buckets=${RACE_BUCKETS:-4} --keys-per-bucket=${RACE_KEYS_PER_BUCKET:-32} --report=report/race.jsonl. Pipes through tee report/race.stdout.log; PIPESTATUS[0] preserves the harness exit code so race-soak exits 0/1/2 per the harness contract. snapshot_host pre-readyz / pre-load / post-load writes df -h / + free -m sections into report/host.txt (free unavailable on macOS gracefully reported). dump_container_logs writes docker logs strata / strata-cassandra / strata-ceph into report/<name>.log; missing-container failures degrade to a warning, never fail the script. Concurrency >64 cap enforced by the harness itself (internal/racetest.MaxConcurrency=64; runner.go rejects with exit 2). Makefile race-soak target depends on `build` so a fresh checkout produces bin/strata-racecheck before invocation. SigV4 creds default to AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY env or strataAK/strataSK fallback so the script Just Works against the dev compose stack (which boots with the same defaults).

userStories.title: Nightly CI workflow on ubuntu-latest
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the race-soak run to execute on a nightly schedule and surface failures via GitHub Actions, without blocking PRs and without paid runners.
acceptanceCriteria: New `.github/workflows/race-nightly.yml` declares `runs-on: ubuntu-latest` (explicit; no larger / self-hosted runner)
`schedule: cron: '0 3 * * *'` (03:00 UTC daily) and `workflow_dispatch:` for manual runs with `duration` + `concurrency` inputs
Job builds strata image via `make docker-build`, brings up `ci`-profile stack via `make up-all-ci`, runs `make race-soak` with RACE_DURATION=1h
Uploads `report/` as `race-nightly-<run-id>` artefact (retain 30 days)
On every run (success OR fail), writes `report/SUMMARY.md` content into $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
On failure, workflow exits non-zero so badge flips red. No email / Slack / auto-issue
Workflow does NOT run on pull_request — only schedule and workflow_dispatch
Workflow timeout = 90 min
`concurrency: race-nightly` group + `cancel-in-progress: true`
Existing ci.yml jobs unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Report parsing + summary
userStories.description: As a maintainer reviewing nightly artefacts, I want a one-page summary at the top of the report folder so I can triage in 30 seconds.
acceptanceCriteria: New `scripts/racecheck/summarize.sh` (POSIX sh + jq) consumes `report/race.jsonl` and prints: total ops by class, op throughput (ops/sec), p50/p95/p99 latency per class, inconsistency count + first-3 examples, host snapshot delta (RSS pre/post, disk pre/post — pulled from report/host.txt)
Summary embedded into `report/SUMMARY.md` and printed in workflow log via `cat report/SUMMARY.md >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`
Script handles empty / partial reports gracefully (process killed mid-run)
Script runs in <5s on a 20 MB JSON-lines report
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Baseline run + ROADMAP close-flip
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a recorded zero-inconsistency baseline run committed to the repo so future regressions are detectable against a known-good shape.
acceptanceCriteria: Trigger nightly workflow manually via workflow_dispatch (or wait for first scheduled run); commit `docs/racecheck/baseline-2026-XX.md` with SUMMARY.md content + commit SHA + workflow link
Commit ROADMAP.md close-flip in same commit per CLAUDE.md Roadmap maintenance rule: `~~**P1 — Race harness as a real test, not a gate.**~~ — **Done.** ... (commit `<pending>`)`
If any inconsistencies are detected, file as P1 entry under Known latent bugs / Correctness & consistency BEFORE flipping; do NOT flip headline if open
If workflow OOMs or stack fails to come up within 10 min: file P2 entry under Consolidation & validation and tune US-005 limits tighter; do not flip headline
Delete `tasks/prd-race-harness.md` per CLAUDE.md PRD lifecycle rule (Ralph snapshot is the canonical record)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
